title: Pronunciation: Na, Ta & Ha Rows
description: Learn the pronunciation rules for the Na-row (な行), Ta-row (た行), and Ha-row (は行).

blocks.title: Key Sounds This Week
points: Ta-row: た (ta), ち (chi), つ (tsu), て (te), と (to) — Note 'chi' and 'tsu'!
Na-row: な (na), に (ni), ぬ (nu), ね (ne), の (no) — Very regular, like 'n' in English.
Ha-row: は (ha), ひ (hi), ふ (fu), へ (he), ほ (ho) — Note 'fu' and the particle 'ha/wa'.
The sound 'tsu' is like the 'ts' in 'cats', NOT 'soo'.
The sound 'fu' is blowing air gently, lighter than an English 'f'.

pairs.diff: Tongue position behind the teeth for 'tsu'.
Don't bite your lip for 'fu' like an English 'f'!

blocks.title: True or False — Check Your Understanding

items.explanation: It's pronounced 'tsu', like the end of 'cats'.

items.statement: The Ha-row changes to B and P with marks.
items.explanation: Yes! は(ha) -> ば(ba) with dakuten, and ぱ(pa) with handakuten (゜).

items.statement: The word for seven is pronounced 'nay-nay'.
